vocab.meaning: sống khép kín, ít giao tiếp với người khác
vocab.example: As a child, I tended to keep to myself and spend hours reading in my room rather than playing outside.
I generally got on with my peers, but I still valued my alone time to explore my own hobbies.

vocab.meaning: trở nên dạn dĩ, cởi mở hơn
vocab.example: It took me a few years of primary school to finally come out of my shell and start enjoying group activities.
I often found it difficult to fit in with the more athletic kids, so I preferred playing solo games.

vocab.meaning: giao lưu, tiếp xúc với nhiều người
vocab.example: My parents always encouraged me to mix with other children at the park to improve my social skills.

vocab.meaning: đi chơi, dành thời gian cùng ai đó
vocab.example: I used to hang out with a small group of close friends instead of being part of a large crowd.

vocab.meaning: bị sa lầy/đắm chìm quá mức vào cái gì
vocab.example: I would often get bogged down in my own imaginative world, completely oblivious to the other kids around me.

vocab.meaning: tham gia vào (một hoạt động đang diễn ra)
vocab.example: Whenever there was a football match, I was always the last one to join in because I was quite shy.
Looking back on my childhood, I realize that I was much more of a lone wolf than a social butterfly.

vocab.meaning: né tránh (vì thiếu tự tin hoặc sợ hãi)
vocab.example: I often shied away from team sports because I was afraid of making mistakes in front of others.
